consensus: Metaswarm is correctly gated behind explicit --metaswarm opt-in across all layers
Anti-silent-downgrade policy consistently documented and instructed to agents
50 SL x 5 W = 250 contract is encoded in commander agent, launcher, and docs
Depth Guard bounded to 3 levels with proper can_launch:false enforcement
Premium model policy explicit in commander agent with banned model list
conflicts: Branch naming inconsistency: README says stampede/{task_id} vs AGENTS says stampede/<agent-id>/<task-id>
Max worker count discrepancy: SKILL.md says max 8, AGENTS.md says max 20
SKILL.md does not name Depth Guard explicitly despite relying on it
SKILL.md has no banned model list - model validation absent at orchestrator level
key_gaps: Launcher does not validate post-run that commanders actually achieved 250 workers
Failed pane starts in launcher only print count but do not mark run as partial
No stuck detection: dead workers leave tasks in claimed/ with no recovery
Smoke test does not cover partial/failed bundle scenarios
Teardown may leave orphan child processes not tracked by PID files
summary: The commander launch profile is well-designed with explicit metaswarm gating, Depth Guard, premium model policy, and anti-downgrade rules. Key implementation gaps: no post-run validation of actual worker counts, no error handling for failed pane creation, and documentation inconsistencies in branch naming and max worker limits.
